grades.expectation: Response identifies 68 years as a critical lease length issue
grades.reason: Clearly identifies 68 years as 'short lease' with significant legal and financial implications, well below the 80-year red line

grades.expectation: Response mentions specific lender names and their minimum requirements (not just 'most lenders want 70+ years')
grades.reason: Names specific lenders (Santander, HSBC, Nationwide) and mentions typical minimum requirements of 70-85 years at start and 25-30 years remaining at end

grades.expectation: Response mentions marriage value applies below 80 years
grades.reason: Extensively explains marriage value applies below 80 years, including the 50% landlord entitlement and cost implications

grades.expectation: Response discusses lease extension options (statutory Section 42, informal, assign benefit)
grades.reason: Details Section 42 statutory right, explains the assignment process to transfer benefit to buyers, and mentions informal options

grades.expectation: Response quantifies the price impact (discount percentage or range)
grades.reason: States short lease properties are typically worth 10-20% less than equivalent long lease properties

grades.expectation: Response mentions reduced buyer pool due to mortgage restrictions
grades.reason: Clearly states buyer pool limited to cash buyers or those using specialist niche lenders with higher rates due to mortgage restrictions
